1998-11-01.title: Yugoslav Wields Ax in 'Pacification' of Academia
1998-11-01.abstract: Serb Pres Slobodan Milosevic is moving to still academic voices he considers troublesome to his regime; Vladimir Vodinelic, law professor at Belgrade University for 27 years, was dismissed four months ago, and blames revival of old Communist category of the politically correct person'; his photo; Milsevic is aided by coalition partners, Vojislav Seselj of far right party and his wife's far-left party; loyalty to triumvirate is now ticket to academic promotion; numerous professors have been ousted or banned from classrooms, with departments reorganized under loyalist deans; academics say Milosevic seems to believe that having given concessions to US on Kosovo he is free to do as he wishes elsewhere (M)

1998-11-01.title: Despite Bluster, Qaddafi Weighs Deal for 2 Bombing Suspects
1998-11-01.abstract: *Libyan regime continues to project defiance, mocking six years of UN sanctions, but Col Muammar el-Qaddafi nevertheless is reported considering deal offered by US and Britain; he would be required to turn over two suspects in bombing of Pan Am flight 103 for trial in Netherlands by Scottish judges, and sanctions would be lifted as soon as two are surrendered; their photos; map (M)

1998-11-01.title: Aid Groups Press U.N. for New Effort to End Sudan's Civil War
1998-11-01.abstract: Private aid groups, currently sending millions of dollars worth of food and medicine to relieve starvation and suffering in Sudan, are pressing the United Nations to make a vigorous new effort to end the nearly 30-year civil war there instead of merely feeding its victims.

1998-11-01.title: U.S. Lawyer A Champion Of Victims In Rwanda
1998-11-01.abstract: Pierre-Richard Prosper discusses his decision to give up comfortable lawyer's life in Los Angeles to work in Tanzania for UN war-crimes tribunal; Prosper has become first prosecutor to get genocide verdict involving killings in Rwanda in 1994, and he also persuaded judges to include rape among crmes constituting genocide; 35-year-old son of Haitian immigrants lived in New York and later worked for Los Angeles district attorney's office prosecuting gang members (S)

1998-11-01.title: King Hussein Ails. His Brother Waits.
1998-11-01.abstract: King Hussein's cancer has inspired those around him in Jordan's ruling circles to plan for seamless transition that king himself envisioned when he named his younger brother, Crown Prince Hassan, as his successor more than three decades ago; 51-year-old Crown Prince, in rare interview, talks about close relationship with king, his aspirations for regional peace and irritation with those who criticize his manner as overly erudite and intellectual; photo; Hassan has been standing in since king went to Mayo Clinic for cancer treatment more than three months ago (M)

1998-11-02.title: After Crackdown, Militant Group Threatens Arafat's Forces
1998-11-02.abstract: Hamas, militant Islamic group, lashes back at crackdown by Palestinian Authority by warning for first time that it might turn guns on Yasir Arafat's police force; Maj Gen Ghazi Jabali, Palestinian chief of police, says his officers will respond, and issues orders to shoot suspected assailants; threat by Hamas contradicts its longstanding position of avoiding civil war; signals deepening crisis with Palestinian Authority following signing of Isralei-Palestinian peace accord; since thwarted suicide bombing against Israeli school bus in Gaza Strip on October 29, Palestinian police have rounded up estimated 300 Hamas followers and placed leader, Sheik Ahmed Yassin, under house arrest (M)

1998-11-02.title: China Blocks Chief of Independent Research Group From Traveling
1998-11-02.abstract: Peng Ming, leader of China Development Union, independent research group closed down by Government, is refused permission to leave China, and his passport is confiscated; is apprehended while trying to cross into Hong Kong; planned to travel to US, where he was invited to speak at Harvard and Columbia universities (M)

1998-11-02.title: Rabin's Lot Is Rancor, In Death As in Life
1998-11-02.abstract: Israel holds first nationwide memorial for Prime Min Yitzhak Rabin, who was assassinated in 1995, signaling concerted effort to transform him from partisan hero into figure of consensus or national symbol of peace; politicians of all stripes gather at his grave in Jerusalem, broadening mourning that, until recently, has been domain of only centrists and leftists; as Israelis vent feelings in discussion groups nationwide, it remains clear that trauma of assassination remainly at least partly undigested and unresolved, as is fate of peace accord with Palestinians signed recently by Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu; photos (M)

1998-11-02.title: The Next Trouble Spot in the Balkans: Montenegro
1998-11-02.abstract: Unrest is building in Yugoslav republic of Montenegro, where Pres Milo Djukanovic and other leaders say they would like to remain part of Yugoslavia, but not if federation with Serbia means submitting to autocracy or Slobodan Milosevic, Yugoslav President; Montenegrins, who for most part have no ethnic or religious quarrels to pick with Serbia, say they hope to create open and democratic society as example of how all Yugoslavs should be able to live; are falling back on tradition of smuggling to achieve goals, arguing that this is only way to subsidize government cut off financially by Belgrade; smuggling has become problem for Western countries that praise Montenegrans for challenging Milosevic; map (M)

1998-11-02.title: Mexico City Journal; Let's All Do Lunch the Modern Way: Make It Fast
1998-11-02.abstract: Residents of Mexico City are abandoning once-sacrosanct tradition of long, leisurely lunches; many have grown too busy, are following international corporate schedule, or consider fast-food dining more efficient; many working-class Mexicans simply can no longer afford slightest culinary extravagance; photo (M)

1998-11-03.title: Cut Prices Or Else, Libya Tells Its Shops
1998-11-03.abstract: Libyan Govt of Col Muammar el-Qaddafi warns shopkeepers to lower prices to officially dictated levels or face violence by dreaded 'purification committees' (M)

1998-11-03.title: Irish Leader Urges Restarting Ulster Peace Drive
1998-11-03.abstract: Prime Min Bertie Ahern of Irish Republic confers with Catholic and Protestant leaders of new Northern Ireland Assembly in an attempt to restart stalled Ulster peace effort, two days after killing of Brian Service, Roman Catholic man, in Belfast; is seeking to allay fears that murder, apparently by Protestant splinter guerrilla group, will lead to renewed violence (M)

1998-11-03.title: OFFICIALS PREDICT HURRICANE'S TOLL WILL EXCEED 7,000
1998-11-03.abstract: Rescue workers throughout Central America struggle to reach scores of remote peasant settlements left battered and isolated by Hurricane Mitch as authorities in Nicaragua and Honduras predict combined death toll may exceed 7,000; focus of Nicaraguan rescue efforts is Posoltega region, located at foot of Casitas volcano, 50 miles northwest of capital of Managua, where 800 people are confirmed dead in mudslides and at least 2,000 are reported missing; Pres Carlos Flores Facusse, in grim television address, describes 'panorama of death, desolation and ruin' throughout entire country; numerous deaths and widespread damage to property and crops also reported in El Salvador and Guatemala; photos; map (M)

1998-11-03.title: French Major Held in Spying At NATO Panel
1998-11-03.abstract: Maj Pierre Bunel, French military officer at NATO headquarters in Brussels, is arrested on suspicion of telling Serbia about targets alliance was planning to bomb if Serbian forces failed to halt attacks against civilians in Kosovo Province; is accused of passing top secret documents to Serbian agents (S)

1998-11-03.title: World Briefs
1998-11-03.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: TO PRINT $759 MILLION IN 2 MONTHS -- Russia plans to print a maximum of $759 million by the end of the year. Deputy Prime Minister Yuri D. Maslyukov said Russian and International Monetary Fund experts had worked out the limit. Another Deputy Prime Minister, Valentina Matvienko, said the Government would not be able to pay all the back wages promised to state workers before the end of the year.   (Agence France-Presse)

1998-11-04.title: With Explosion in Data Networking, Companies Readjust Strategies to Meet New Demands : Equipment Makers Race to Get Right Products
1998-11-04.abstract: LONDON—  Ever since the introduction of the World Wide Web began triggering explosive growth of Internet use earlier this decade, technology gurus have been predicting a convergence between conventional voice telephony and the rapidly evolving market for transmitting data.

1998-11-04.title: Indonesian Army Is Blamed For Role in Spring Violence
1998-11-04.abstract: Report by fact-finding team accuses members of Indonesian military of helping to instigate violence and looting in capital of Jakarta in May that killed nearly 1,200 people and led to resignation of President Suharto (S)

1998-11-04.title: Middle East Accord in Peril As Both Sides Appeal to the U.S.
1998-11-04.abstract: Israel and Palestinians appeal to US to salvage land-for-security peace accord, which has been severely tested by pattern of postponements by Israel's Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu, who has again delayed presenting it Cabinet for ratification because of persistent pressure from right-wingers; he is demanding that US wrest from Palestinians written timetable for arresting 30 fugitives wanted for terrorism; Palestinians accuse Netanyahu of political brinksmanship; accuse Israel of taking advantage of delays to establish footholds for new settlements on West Bank, which would be illegal once agreement takes effect; implore US to compel him to move beyond impasse; Statye Dept spokesman says Palestinians have submitted acceptable security plan, meeting their obligations for accord to take effect, and that US believes there is no reason to delay implementation; photo (M)

1998-11-04.title: Spain Making Contact With Basque Rebels
1998-11-04.abstract: Spain's Prime Minister, Jose Maria Aznar, authorizes Govt to talk with people close to leading Basque guerrilla group to test trustworthiness of rebels, who recently announced indefinite cease-fire; has said that peace talks cannot be held directly with rebel group until it permanently renounces violence (M)

1998-11-04.title: Popular Ex-President Announces Plans to Run Again in Nigeria
1998-11-04.abstract: Gen Olsegun Abasanjo, President of Nigeria from 1976 to 1979 who is country's only military ruler to relinquish power to civilian Govt, announces plans to run again and is immediately regarded by many Nigerians as front-runner in elections set for February 1999; emerged from three yers in prison in June, shortly after death of dictator, Gen Sani Abacha; pledge by military Govt to hand power over to civilian President in May 1999 noted (M)

1998-11-05.title: French Youths Strike for the Right to Study Hard
1998-11-05.abstract: Thousands of the 2.3 million high school students in France plan to resume protests against shortage of teachers and shortcomings in curriculum with demonstration in Paris; but at Lycee Gaston Bachelard in Paris suburb of Chelles, as in other schools across the country, students are torn about how hard to keep pushing; map (M)

1998-11-05.title: Netanyahu, After Delay, Will Offer Wye Pact to Cabinet
1998-11-05.abstract: Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu is set to present to Israeli cabinet West Bank withdrawal agreement reached last month with Yasir Arafat; Netanyahu has been delaying deal, which was reached at talks with Pres Clinton, because of dispute over when Palestinians would arrest 30 people wanted by Israel on charges of involvement in the deaths of Israelis; Arafat says 12 of the 30 have been arrested and rest are being pursued (M)

1998-11-05.title: U.S. Enlists Russians to Spot Nuclear Smuggling
1998-11-05.abstract: United States, in effort to prevent nuclear weapons and materials from being smuggled out of Russia, announces plans to train Russian customs agents in identifying and arresting smugglers; US Customers Service plan includes training Russian agents at Pacific Northwest National Laboratory, Engergy Department's nuclear reservation in Paso, Wash (M)

1998-11-05.title: Israeli Diplomat Urges Vatican to Delay Beatification of Pius XII
1998-11-05.abstract: Israeli Ambassador to Vatican, Aharon Lopez, urges it to wait 50 years before moving ahead with any plan to beatify Pope Pius XII, who has been criticized by Jewish groups and others for remaining silent during Holocaust (M)

1998-11-05.title: SAUDI IS INDICTED IN BOMB ATTACKS ON U.S. EMBASSIES
1998-11-05.abstract: Federal grand jury in Manhattan returns 238-count indictment charging Saudi exile Osama bin Laden in bombings of two US Embassies in Kenya and Tanzania in Aug and with conspiring to commit other acts of terrorism against Americans abroad; Government officials offer two rewards of $5 million each for information leading to arrest or conviction of bin Laden and Muhammad Atef, his chief military commander; prosecutors also unseal earlier indictment, issued in June, that includes similar but less detailed charges against bin Laden; new indictment accuses him of leading vast terrorist conspiracy from 1989 to present, in which he is said to have been working in concert with Sudan, Iraq and Iran and terrorist groups to build weapons and attack US military installations; photos (M)

1998-11-06.title: Long Dispute Ends as Berlin Court Backs Islamic School Lessons
1998-11-06.abstract: Berlin court rules that city's 35,000 Muslim children will be allowed to receive Islamic religious instruction in schools for first time; decision reflects Germany's gradual and painful reconciliation with fact that it is multi-ethnic state with large population of foreigners (M)

1998-11-06.title: U.S. Diplomat Pleads for Political Solution to Civil War in Congo
1998-11-06.abstract: Assistant Sec of State Susan E Rice ends weeklong trip to Africa by urging political solution to civil war in Congo, which has drawn in neighboring countries and threatens to widen into larger regional conflict (M)

1998-11-06.title: Brazil's Austerity Plan Clears Important Hurdle on Pension Cost
1998-11-06.abstract: Brazilian Congress approves measures intended to reduce imbalances between its public and private pension systems, a major element of Government's austerity plan and critical step in restoring confidence of foreign investors and international monetary fund (M)

1998-11-06.title: Malaysia to Be Unlikely Host at Economic Meeting
1998-11-06.abstract: Upcoming Asia Pacific Economic Cooperation meeting in Malaysia could hardly be held in a more provocative place; Malysia recently imposed sweeping controls on flow of capital in its stock and currency markets, particularly on investment from overseas; in doing so, Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad has in effect slammed the door on global economy that Pres Clinton and other leaders are coming to Malaysia to champion; photo (M)

1998-11-06.title: Exiles Sue Castro in Spain
1998-11-06.abstract: Foundation for Human Rights in Cuba, an exile group in Miami, files suit in Spain against Pres Fidel Castro for human rights abuses (S)

1998-11-07.title: World Briefing
1998-11-07.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: CRITICIZED ON ECONOMIC RETREAT -- Deputy Secretary of State Strobe Talbott attacked Russia's retreat from free-market economics and warned that financial decline could lead to ''political drift, turmoil and even crack up'' there. Help from the International Monetary Fund, Mr. Talbott said in a speech at Stanford University, ''must wait until the Russian Government shows itself willing and able to make the difficult structural adjustments necessary for recovery and growth.'''   (Reuters)

1998-11-07.title: Food Crisis Forces Russia To Swallow Its Pride
1998-11-07.abstract: Burning question for Kremlin these days is food; with winter fast approaching, nation is locked in primal struggle to feed its citizens and prevent them from freezing; it is race against time; food reserves are dwindling; remote cities are left without heat; and huge logistical task of moving grain, meat and fuel is well behind schedule; it is a mark of Russia's anxiety that Prime Min Yevgeny M Primakov's aides put aside its pride to buy $625 million worth of grain, meat, soybeans and other food from United States; photo (M)

1998-11-07.title: Chase Reviews Nazi-Era Role
1998-11-07.abstract: Chase Manhattan Corp says that it is investigating allegations that it froze accounts of Jewish customers in Paris during Nazi occupation of France before formal orders to freeze accounts were issued; Chase, one of largest American banks, says it is also examining whether its Paris office was 'overly cooperative' in providing banking services to Germany during occupation and whether assets seized by Nazis and Vichy Govt were ever returned (S)

1998-11-07.title: World War I Sets Off A New Battle In France
1998-11-07.abstract: A day after France's Socialist Prime Minister called for the rehabilitation of French soldiers executed for refusing to fight in a World War I battle that cost 300,000 French lives, the country's Gaullist conservative President, Jacques Chirac, today called the Prime Minister's remarks ''inopportune.''    The 1917 battle, along a ridge road called the Chemin des Dames, remains a sensitive subject in France as it prepares to celebrate the 80th anniversary of the Nov. 11 armistice that ended the war.

1998-11-07.title: Goma Journal; One Rebellion Too Many for War-Weary Congolese
1998-11-07.abstract: People in Goma, Congo, rebels' home base, express skepticism, war-weariness and outright hostility at another rebellion, second one in two years, when last one produced so little to improve their lives; they say rebel troops are corrupt, and that in reality, rebellion is 'a foreigners' movement'; photo; map (M)

1998-11-08.title: Facing Oblivion, Rust-Belt Giants Top Russian List Of Vexing Crises
1998-11-08.abstract: Most vexing of Russia's problems lie in its Rust Belt, interlocking series of industrial giants now living off barter and choking on bloated work force; many of industries are pillars of communities that have no other reason for being; solving their problems without causing social upheaval will be key test for Russia's new Government, headed by Prime Min Yevgeny M Primakov and staffed with old-style Soviet ministers who have pointedly set out to rescue Russian industry; many fault Pres Boris N Yeltsin's team of young free-market reformers for failing to anticipate economic disarray--and corruption--that followed their lifting of state controls; Magnitogorsk, once largest steel mill in former Soviet Union, is producing less than half raw steel it did decade ago, with same work force; bottom has fallen out of its domestic market as Russian factories that buy steel approach bankruptcy and struggle to sell what Russians either do not want or cannot afford; photos; map; charts (L)

1998-11-08.title: Rising Russian Imports Fuel Dispute With U.S. Steel Makers
1998-11-08.abstract: Russia and United States are engaged in trade war over hot-rolled steel; American steel makers brought anti-dumping case in Washington against Russia, Japan and Brazil, contending that their foreign competitors are selling steel below cost in United States, at grave damage to domestic industry; Russia is not biggest exporter of hot-rolled steel to US, but Russian producers have been squeezed by drop in domestic demand and collapse of their markets in Southeast Asia, and they have stepped up their shipments to US; imports of Russian steel rose to 2.2 million tons in first seven months of 1998; in Magnitogorsk, heart of Russian steel industry, exports to US are matter of survival (M)

1998-11-08.title: Etched in Stone, Words From Great War's Dead
1998-11-08.abstract: Chemin des Dames quarries in eastern France bear German, American and French World War I graffiti; nearby Fort Malmaison in Braye-en-Laonnnois was held by German invaders from 1914 to 1917 against countless French attacks; area is still marked by artillery shell craters and trench lines; World War I buffs digging near underground limestone quarries found evidecne left by soldiers of three nations who had fought one another in subterranean labybrinth for three years; photo; map (M)

1998-11-08.title: For a Day, Russians Are United In Nostalgia
1998-11-08.abstract: Revolution Day, formerly premier feast of Soviet Communism, is commemorated by 10,000 people who march through center of Moscow; turnout is better than in recent years, reflecting impact of latest economic crisis; in old Soviet days, Nov 7 was marked with military parade with huge banners proclaiming Soviet achievement and might (M)

1998-11-08.title: On Piping Out Caspian Oil, U.S. Insists the Cheaper, Shorter Way Isn't Better
1998-11-08.abstract: Huge amounts of oil and gas are believed to lie beneath Caspian Sea, and three new nations that share its shoreline--Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan and Turkmenistan--stand to reap billions of dollars; because Caspian region is landlocked, pipelines must be built to carry its oil to world markets; oil companies will soon announce which route they prefer for their main export pipeline from Baku in Azerbaijan; United States and many other nations believe decision will have profound economic and geopolitical consequences, and are lobbying intensely for their favored routes; Clinton Administration wants oil companies to build their pipeline across Georgia and Turkey; that route would promote American interests in region, but would be far more costly to construct than other routes being considered; Russia and Iran are pushing routes of their own, urging companies not to commit themselves to American-backed one; photos; map (L)

1998-11-09.title: Jailed Israelis: A Spy Fiasco Or a Warning By Cyprus?
1998-11-09.abstract: Two Isrealis, Udi Hargov and Yigal Damary, are arrested on suspicion of spying on military installation in Cyprus, raising questions in Israel about whether it is latest in series of bungles by Israeli intelligence agency, Mossad; arrest spotlights increased regional tensions about growing security cooperation between Israel and Turkey (M)

1998-11-09.title: As Dalai Lama Woos Beijing, Tibetans Pray for His Return
1998-11-09.abstract: Tibetans nurture fervent desire to see Dalai Lama, their exiled god and king, return to Potala Palace, and political atmosphere in China seems more open to negotiations on issue than at any time in a decade; Dalai Lama is proposing new initiative that he hopes will lead to talks with Chinese leaders, and he is expected seek support for it when he meets with Vice Pres Al Gore during his current visit to Washington; map; photo (M)

1998-11-09.title: 2 Asian Tigers Gird for Battle to Get Lion's Share of Financial Trading
1998-11-09.abstract: HONG KONG—  A battle is intensifying between two of Asia's largest financial centers as a rapidly liberalizing Singapore and an increasingly interventionist Hong Kong seek to gain the upper hand in lucrative financial sector trading.

1998-11-09.title: Turin Journal; The Militant Epicures: Even the Barricades Groan
1998-11-09.abstract: Over 100,000 people are expected to attend five-day Slow Food festival in Turin, Italy, celebrating languourous enjoyment of slowly prepared regional delicacies and demonstrating their disdain for fast food; Slow Food movement was begun 13 years ago by journalist Carlo Petrini and group of leftist intellectuals; has become fashionable in Italy and throughout Europe, with 40,000 members in 35 countries; it is catching on via Internet with American food and wine connoisseurs; photos (M)

1998-11-09.title: Pinochet Vows to Fight 'With All My Spirit'
1998-11-09.abstract: Gen Augusto Pinochet, former Chilean dictator, says he is 'hurt and bewildered' by his arrest in London, and he vows to fight attempts to extradite him to Spain to stand trial; photo (M)

1998-11-10.title: Toronto Journal; Canadian Fashion: Doctors in Bulletproof Vests
1998-11-10.abstract: Interview with Dr Henry Morgentaler, Canada's most famous and vilified doctor performing abortions, who refuses to be silenced in wake of Buffalo, NY, slaying of Dr Barnett Slepian; murder has made clear that shootings of three Canadian doctors who performed abortions were linked and focused attention on Morgentaler; photo (M)

1998-11-10.title: Local Elections in Israel: A Virtual Free-for-All
1998-11-10.abstract: Local election campaigns have been particularly down and dastardly in Israel this season, with far more violence than in past; ethnicity, religious-secular divides, clan rivalries and municipal scandals inflame passions; dirty tricks are commonplace; map; photo (M)

1998-11-10.title: Lord Hunt, 88, Everest Trek Leader, Dies
1998-11-10.abstract: Lord Hunt of Llanfairwaterdine, who led expedition in 1953 in which Sir Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay became first people to reach top of Mount Everest, dies at age 88; photo (M)

1998-11-10.title: Pinochet Lawyer Asks Britain To Recognize Chile's Wishes
1998-11-10.abstract: Gen Augusto Pinochet's lawyer, tells Britain's highest court that his detention threatens 'delicate balance' between 'interests of justice and state stability' in Chile; holds Chile has made its peace with former dictator (M)

1998-11-10.title: PRI Reverses Fortunes and Wins Crucial Mexican Governorships
1998-11-10.abstract: Mexican Pres Ernesto Zedillo's governing party wins gubernatorial races in Sinaloa and Puebla and may have regained municipal control over three state capitals that had fallen to opposition, closing out election year in which it has repeatedly humbled bickering opposition rivals; results fuel party's hopes of extending its 70-year domination of Mexican politics; leftist coalition wins governorship of Tlaxcala (M)

1998-11-11.title: Mexican Official Links Massacre To Drug Rivalry
1998-11-11.abstract: Gen Jose Luis Chavez says rival marijuna-smuggling gang led by Lino Quintana was responsible for Sept massacre of 19 men, women and children at home of Fermin Castro, near Ensenada, Mex, news conference; says Quintana and his armed colleagues were drunk and high on cocaine when their talks with Castro turned violent; three people have been arrested; Quintana remains at large (M)

1998-11-11.title: Argentina Hears Rumors Of a Royal British Suitor
1998-11-11.abstract: Rumors of blooming romance between British Prince Andrew and Argentine Pres Carlos Saul Menem's daughter, Zulema Maria Eva Menem, while most likely highly exaggerated, are being taken in Argentina as sign that two nations are finally putting 1982 Falkland Islands war behind them; Pres Menem apparently encourages gossip; photo (M)

1998-11-11.title: In Germany, Wal-Mart Touches Off a Price War
1998-11-11.abstract: FRANKFURT—  Unhappy German merchants already felt squeezed by a six-year slump before a fearsome new rival threatened to make things worse.

1998-11-11.title: Dalai Lama Tells Clinton of Chinese Balkiness
1998-11-11.abstract: Dalai Lama, Tibet's exiled spiritual leader, meets with Pres Clinton, Washington, DC; presses for talks with China aimed at gaining some autonomy for Tibet; United States says Clinton agrees with him on need for direct talks; he also meets with Vice Pres Al Gore, Hillary Rodham Clinton and State Sec Madeleine K Albright (M)

1998-11-11.title: Brazilians Scale Back Pledges to Cut Budget
1998-11-11.abstract: Brazil, which pledged to slash discretionary spending by $7 billion in effort to bolster its economic credibility, releases revised budget figures showing true cuts will be about half of promised amount; total spending will actually rise in 1999 (M)

1998-11-12.title: Argentina Joins War On Global Warming
1998-11-12.abstract: In an important breakthrough, Argentina signaled that it would become the first developing country to adopt binding targets for controlling emissions of industrial waste gases that are believed to cause global warming. Until now, developing countries had presented a united front in resisting specific limits on their emissions of such gases, which are produced by the burning of coal, oil, wood and natural gas. Developed countries had agreed last December in Kyoto, Japan, to reduce emissions by an average of about 5 percent below 1990 levels in the period 2008 to 2012. However, poorer nations have refused to follow suit until industrial countries actually show results.    Article, page A7.

1998-11-12.title: U.S. Calls Israeli Vote 'Step Forward' to Peace
1998-11-12.abstract: Sec of State Madeleine K Albright says Israeli Cabinet's approval of Middle East accord is 'important step forward in the peace process' (M)

1998-11-12.title: Germany and U.S. Nurture Bond At a Mansion With a Few Ghosts
1998-11-12.abstract: New American Academy opens in Berlin mansion that was once wartime home of Hitler's chief economic adviser, Walther Funk; much of founding grant for academy was provided by descendants of Hans Arnhold, Jewish banker who fled mansion; former Sec of State Henry A Kissinger attends opening (M)

1998-11-12.title: U.N. Panel to Visit Cambodia to Study Khmer Rouge Atrocities
1998-11-12.abstract: Three legal experts appointed by Sec Gen Kofi Annan will go to Cambodia to study documents on Khmer Rouge atrocities and recommend how and where tribunal should judge those crimes; legal panel consists of Sir Ninian Stephen, Steven Ratner and Rajsoomer Lallah (M)

1998-11-12.title: March of the Sadly Bilked
1998-11-12.abstract: Some two hundred people who have lost money in multimillion-dollar futures-market scam hold protest in Beijing, China (S)

1998-11-13.title: Japan Moves to Thwart APEC's Free-Trade Plan
1998-11-13.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  Japan has mustered enough support from other Asian and Pacific countries hit hard by the region's economic crisis to derail a "fast-track" trade-liberalization plan that Washington says is critical if it is to combat protectionism in America, officials said Thursday.

1998-11-13.title: New Policy Planning Chief
1998-11-13.abstract: Morton H Halperin, former White House and Pentagon official, will become director of policy planning at State Department (S)

1998-11-13.title: You Really Can't Get a Cab When Rome Proposes Reforms
1998-11-13.abstract: Taxicab drivers strike in Rome, charging that mayor's proposal to allow them to set their own prices and hours would impoverish them; strike highlights attachment to statist status quo, even as country tries to liberalize economy by easing crippling bureaucracy and inflexibility in work force (S)

1998-11-13.title: Paine Journal; Pinochet's Arrest Revives Buried Fears in Chile
1998-11-13.abstract: Arrest of Gen Augusto Pinochet in London, on charges of human rights violations, revives deadly political grudges in Paine, town of grape and citrus growers in Chile; photo; map; mothers and sisters of men who disappeared after coup against Salvador Allende in 1973 speak bitterly of their loss and humiliation at hands of former soldiers and paramilitaries; Paine's turmoil began in late 1960's when landless farmers squatted on unused ranch land; invasions increased when Allende came to power in 1970 and socialist activists helped peasants organize cooperatives; movement came to violent end in 1973 (Paine Journal) (M)

1998-11-13.title: Israel Calmly Readies Itself, Just in Case
1998-11-13.abstract: Israelis calmly pick up gas masks in case of Iraqi missile attack; people in Ramat Gan, which was hit hard by Iraqi missiles during Persian Gulf war, comment on latest crisis; Israeli officials are keeping low profile, avoiding threats of retaliation even as US prepares for possible military strike (S)

1998-11-14.title: World Briefing
1998-11-14.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: TROOP REDUCTION IN ULSTER  Because of the improving security situation, British troop levels in Northern Ireland are being reduced to 15,000, 2,000 fewer than this summer and the smallest number since 1970. Chief Constable Ronnie Flanagan of the Royal Ulster Constabulary said that 400 soldiers would be going back to mainland Britain and that checkpoints near the main airfield were being dismantled.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

1998-11-14.title: Student Protest in Indonesia Explodes in Violence, and 8 Are Killed
1998-11-14.abstract: Violence erupts at student demonstration in Jakarta, killing at least eight people, as Indonesian Parliament votes to hold general elections next spring and to begin reforms; photos (M)

1998-11-14.title: Divided and Distracted, APEC Prepares to Meet
1998-11-14.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  As the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation forum prepares to admit three new members on Saturday, before heading into its annual summit meeting next week, the group appears more divided and distracted than at any time since it was formed in 1989.

1998-11-14.title: Request to Alter Space Station Orbit Is Dropped
1998-11-14.abstract: NASA and Russian space agency agree to set aside last-minute Russian request to launch new international space station into an orbit closer to 12-year-old Mir station than previously planned; technical difficulties cited (M)

1998-11-14.title: Saudi Was Target In Raid, U.S. Says
1998-11-14.abstract: Clinton Administration officials now acknowledge that one of the clear but unstated objectives of last August's raid in Afghanistan was to kill Osama bin Laden, the Saudi exile who has been charged by Federal prosecutors with leading a global war against American interests and who has been linked to the Aug. 7 bombing of two American embassies in East Africa.

1998-11-15.title: Ethnic Albanians Recount Massacre of a Family in Kosovo
1998-11-15.abstract: Article describes massacre in Kosovo province of 21 members of ethnic Albanian family by Serbian forces; massacre of Delijaj clan occurred during fierce guerrilla resistance to onslaught by Serbian tanks, artillery and infantry near village of Gornje Obrinje; maps; photo (M)

1998-11-15.title: U.S. Says It Was Just Hours Away From Starting Attack Against Iraq
1998-11-15.abstract: US reportedly called off huge campaign of air strikes against Iraq after last-minute flurry of diplomatic activity resulted in Iraq's decision to allow UN arms inspectors to resume work; was hours away from first wave of attacks, which would have involved hundreds of cruise missiles launched from ships and B-52 bombers, as well as bombing of Iraqi targets by jet fighters; US officials say attack was aborted because of Iraqi Govt's positive response to last-ditch plea from UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan (M)

1998-11-15.title: U.S. REJECTS PLAN
1998-11-15.abstract: Iraq's Pres Saddam Hussein appears to back down in face of imminent US military attack and says Iraq will resume cooperation with UN arms inspectors; letter to UN Security Council sets conditions on agreement, restating demands that have already been rejected by US, Britain and other Council members; is again rejected as inadequate by US, and American forces remain poised to strike; most Council members say they are prepared to take Hussein at his word; urge quick resumption of arms inspections; Great Britain remains strong in support of US, even though delegates suggest London might be willing to give Iraq chance to re-admit inspectors; Prime Min Tony Blair says until Iraq's compliance is unconditional, British forces will remain on alert; photo (M)

1998-11-15.title: AIDS Stalking Africa's Struggling Economies
1998-11-15.abstract: Special report, Dead Zones, on effects of Africa's AIDS epidemic on continents already shaky economies; epidemic is robbing Africa of much of its potential, striking first at educated its urban professionals, who are backbone of economic expansion; damage is immeasurable since it appears in ways that cannot be seen--namely, businesses that will never be founded, ideas that will never be advanced and university departments that will never be created; in eastern and southern Africa where epidemic is worst, infection rates vary between 10 and 25 percent and virtually all will die within 10 years; photos; charts; map; graph (L)

1998-11-15.title: Deadline Set To Form Rules For Reducing Gas Emissions
1998-11-15.abstract: Negotiators from more than 150 countries meeting in Buenos Aires set deadline of two years for adopting operational rules for cutting emissions of waste and industrial gases believed to cause global warming (M)

1998-11-16.title: A Truckload of 'Howevers' Burdens the Iraqis' Promise
1998-11-16.abstract: Iraqi representative at United Nations Nizar Hamdoon, is pressed to promise that no sites, including Presidential properties belonging to Saddam Hussein and his security services will be barred to inspectors as in past; says that certain 'modalities' govern searches of such places, according to agreement signed with Sec Gen Kofi Annan in Feb (M)

1998-11-16.title: Opponents Find That Ousting Hussein Is Easier Said Than Done
1998-11-16.abstract: Pres Clinton all but calls for coup against Saddam Hussein, vowing to work with Iraqi opposition groups until 'a new government' takes power in Iraq (M)

1998-11-16.title: Albright Visits With Wife Of Dissident In Malaysia
1998-11-16.abstract: Sec of State Madeleine K Albright meets in Kuala Lumpur with Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, wife of jailed Malaysian political leader Anwar Ibrahim, in gesture of solidarity that angers her hosts and suggests that politics could swamp economics at meeting of Asian and Pacific Rim countries; photo (M)

1998-11-16.title: CLINTON ACCEPTS IRAQ'S PROMISE TO ALLOW WEAPONS INSPECTIONS
1998-11-16.abstract: Pres Clinton says that Pres Saddam Hussein of Iraq has unconditionally retreated and once again vowed complete cooperation with United Nations weapons inspectors; says US and British forces that nearly struck Iraq on Nov 14 will remain poised to act; says Iraq must live up to its obligations; calls for overthrow of Hussein; says he is threat to peace of his region and security of world; Pentagon says it is suspending its military buildup in Persian Gulf region; senior US officials say Iraqis have clarified their position in two letters that eliminated all unacceptable conditions; photo (M)

1998-11-16.title: A Trial Will Test China's Grip on the Internet
1998-11-16.abstract: Forthcoming trial of Lin Hai, computer executive, heralds new electronic battleground for China's political dissidents and security forces determined to preserve Communist Party control; Lin is charged with 'inciting subversion of state power' by giving tens of thousands of Chinese E-mail addresses to hostile foreign publications (M)

1998-11-17.title: From the Brink to the Blink: Washington's Iraq Weekend
1998-11-17.abstract: Reconstruction of weekend's events based on interviews with several participants indicates Pres Clinton called off missile attack on Iraq only minutes before it was set to begin on advice of national security adviser Samuel R Berger; Berger apparently responded to CNN report that Iraqis sent letter to United Nations agreeing to let arms inspectors resume work; Defense Sec William S Cohen apparently opposed delaying attack, holding Iraqi letter was too late and too confused; aides to State Sec Madeleine K Albright, who was en route to Asian economic summit conference, said she wanted attack to proceed; photo (M)

1998-11-17.title: South Africa's Press to Face Bias Inquiry
1998-11-17.abstract: South Africa's Human Rights Commission says it will open probe into racism in press, using its powers of subpoena and arrest if needed; commission head, Barney Pityana, says probe is aimed at spurring debate about nature, meaning and incidence of racism he holds is endemic in nation; some editors see carefully timed effort to keep news organizations from criticizing Pres Nelson Mandela's Government on eve of national elections (M)

1998-11-17.title: Fugitive Kurdish Rebel Is a Headache for Italy
1998-11-17.abstract: Fugitive Kurdish Rebel Is a Headache for Italy**Italian Prime Min Massimo D'Alema, in first policy test, is under pressure from leftists in his coalition to offer political asylum to Kurdish rebel leader Abdullah Ocalan, arrested recently at Rome airport; Ocalan is sought in Turkey as terrorist and Germany on murder charges; D'Alema notes Italian law bars extradition of suspects to nations that have death penalty; refuses to pressure from Turkish prison inmates who took Italian prisoner hostage to press for extradition; Turkey says it will soon abolish capital punishment (M)

1998-11-17.title: Report on McCain's Remarks About Tip-Off Angers Paris
1998-11-17.abstract: French Min Hubert Vedrine reacts angrily to televised remarks by Sen John McCain he sees as charge France tipped off Iraq that American bombers were on their way; McCain denies directly accusing France; incident highlights longstanding differences between France and United States over Iraq (M)

1998-11-17.title: Taking Up Crucial Relief Efforts
1998-11-17.abstract: United Nations workers begin heading back to Iraq after latest confrontation; 150 relief workers who had been staying in Jordan return to posts; seek to untie logjams at border crossings caused by their departure that threaten to disrupt internationally monitored food shipments (M)

1998-11-18.title: Kuwaitis Are Puzzled By Lack of U.S. Action
1998-11-18.abstract: Kuwaitis, who feel much affection for United States, regarding US as their liberator and protector after events of 1990, are puzzled by lack of US action now against Iraq; yet another peaceful end over weekend to showdown between Iraq and United States and Britain does little to dispel conspiracy theory held by many people in Kuwait and in other Arab countries that Pres Saddam Hussein of Iraq gives the West, and especially United States, an excuse to keep military presence in Persian Gulf to protect oil supplies (M)

1998-11-18.title: Without Joy, Netanyahu Wins Vote to Adopt Peace Agreement
1998-11-18.abstract: Israeli Parliament approves American-brokered peace plan by significant majority, reflecting widespread, pragmatic acceptance of partioning the Land of Israel; Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu, whose Likud Party was created to defend Jewish homeland from territorial compromise, turns his back on his party's founding credo; in doing so, he wins far more substantial victory, with 75 yes votes, than Labor Party, with its slim margin of support, won for original Israeli-Palestinian peace accord signed in Oslo in 1993; but he does so with Labor opposition's support; only half of his coalition Govt votes for agreement; two of his Cabinet members vote against it; and five ministers, all Likud members, walk out of chambers, refusing to vote; photo of Arab legislator and member of National Religious Party arguing over West Bank map (M)

1998-11-18.title: The 'Lifestyle' Phenomenon:Pills to Enhance Well-Being
1998-11-18.abstract: PARIS—  Disease, pain and life-threatening conditions have been the usual reasons people turn to prescription drugs. But aging baby boomers, interested in continuing to feel and look good as long as possible, have recently fueled a vigorous new phenomenon in the pharmaceutical industry going by the name of "quality of life" or "lifestyle" drugs.

1998-11-18.title: Mergers Give Industry Boost in Developing New Products
1998-11-18.abstract: LONDON—  To an industry afflicted with excess production capacity, a fragmented market and unforgiving regulatory regimes that put pressure on prices, corporate deal-making has been a useful tonic. The logic of combining operations has been compelling enough to generate more than $150 billion of mergers and acquisitions among pharmaceutical companies in the last six years.

1998-11-18.title: Disarmament of the I.R.A. Losing Some of Its Priority in Ulster
1998-11-18.abstract: British and Irish Govts, in gradual but distinct change in policy, are relaxing pressure on Irish Republican Army to disarm; operation of new Northern Ireland Assembly has been thwarted by disarmament issue since Assembly was set up by peace agreement approved in spring; comments on disarmament issue by Irish Prime Min Bertie Ahern and Mo Mowlam, Northern Ireland Sec, noted (M)

1998-11-19.title: Tip Off Iraq? France Says It Couldn't, And Wouldn't
1998-11-19.abstract: Senior French officials say France was not told by US or by Britain precisely when two countries planned to attack Iraq and could not have tipped of Baghdad; suspicions were raised that Iraqis learned of imminence of attack, whereupon they sent letter pledging to allow arms inspectors to begin work once again (M)

1998-11-19.title: Beijing to Expel Foreign Reporter, 2d in a Month
1998-11-19.abstract: China expels Jurgen Kremb, reporter for German news magazine Der Spiegel, one day after eight officials burst into his office and accused him of possessing secret documents; Japanese reporter was charged with possessing state secrets and deported in Oct (M)

1998-11-19.title: West's Medicine Is Raising Bills For China's Sick
1998-11-19.abstract: Imported Western pharmaceuticals have taken China by storm, straining pocketbooks as they hold out promises of cure; Western drug companies have been aggressively courting China's doctors and hospitals not only with new pills, but also with gifts, money and even kickbacks, practices that are banned in US; their goal: to gain access to China's 1.2 billion potentially ailing citizens, most with seemingly unshakable belief in curative power of name-brand Western pills; doctors say that Western medicines and medical equipment have contributed to medical costs that are rising 35 percent a year in China; photo (M)

1998-11-19.title: North Korea Offers U.S. Access to Secret Plant, at a Steep Price
1998-11-19.abstract: Clinton Administration officials say North Korea has demanded that US pay hundreds of millions of dollars for right to inspect huge underground center that US intelligence analysts fear houses nuclear-weapons program; say US has rejected request (M)

1998-11-19.title: Rancorous Asia Pacific Talks End With a Pledge and a Sigh
1998-11-19.abstract: Leaders of 21 Pacific Rim countries wrap up contentious summit meeting in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, by pledging to revive region that has been set back decades by Asian economic crisis; US and Malaysia engaged in war of words during forum over speech by Vice Pres Al Gore; he accused Malaysia of suppressing freedom and threw his support behind protesters who are demanding ouster of Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad (M)

1998-11-20.title: WORLD BRIEFING
1998-11-20.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: MOVE TO CREATE WAR-CRIMES TRIBUNAL -- A Foreign Office official, Tony Lloyd, said that on Nov. 30 Britain will sign a treaty at the United Nations setting up an international war-crimes tribunal. The United States has opposed the treaty, saying it gives the new court too much prosecutorial power and leaves American troops and citizens vulnerable to potentially unfair prosecution.   (AP)    BRITAIN: CRACKDOWN IN TAX HAVENS -- Home Secretary  Jack Straw laid out a plan to crack down on tax-dodging and  money-laundering in the multibillion-dollar offshore tax havens of the Channel Islands and the Isle of Man. A retired Treasury official who helped draft the plan, Andrew Edwards, suggested that assets of the super-rich who were suspected of stacking up illegal gains and who could not account for their offshore wealth should be confiscated.   (Agence France-Presse)

1998-11-20.title: Japan's Ruling Party Agrees To Coalition With Archrival
1998-11-20.abstract: Japanese politics takes turn to the usual as governing Liberal Democratic Party opts for political expedience and agrees to form coalition Govt with its archenemy, Ichiro Ozawa, and his Liberal Party; alliance will strengthen governing party's sway in Parliament, mollify its restive hard-liners and give it sure support in coming budget debates (M)

1998-11-20.title: Berlin Debates Spielberg Holocaust Video Offer
1998-11-20.abstract: Jewish Museum in Berlin begins talks with Steven Spielberg over possibility of housing his vast video history of Holocaust, as debate intensifies over how to recall Hitler's destruction of European Jewry in emerging new center of Berlin; museum is to open in 2000 (M)

1998-11-20.title: Israel's Right Wing Turns From Fury to Resignation
1998-11-20.abstract: Signing of Wye peace memorandum by Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu drops like missile of doom on Israel's political right wing; shatters alliances, blurring political identity of movement built around defending Jewish homeland against territorial compromise; expands political center, and transforms those who clung to their right-wing ideology into a kind of fringe; what has emerged most powerfully is a new pragmatism, which puts settlers' guiding ideology on back burner, if not in waste bin; Netanyahu, who campaigned as hard-line ideologue, and Foreign Min Ariel Sharon, champion of new settlements, set the course, but leaders of settlers and even right-wing National Religious Party followed; photo (L)

1998-11-20.title: U.S. Tobacco Industry Looks Longingly at Chinese Market, but in Vain
1998-11-20.abstract: Business analysts say that as cigarette sales threaten to decline precipitously in United States, American tobacco companies are are counting on China as land of extraordinary potential for growth; but American companies' plans are being thwarted by China's Govt monopoly (M)

1998-11-21.title: Calcutta Journal; Living Up to Mother Teresa's Legacy
1998-11-21.abstract: Sister Nirmala, who succeeded Mother Teresa as head of Missionaries of Charity in Calcutta, India, says she cannot replace Mother Teresa, only try to carry on her work; she is administering Missionaries' ascetic empire while also gathering evidence of miracles that will be needed to win sainthood for Mother Teresa from Vatican; photos (M)

1998-11-21.title: Jakarta Troops Stand Aside as Students Mourn Protesters' Deaths
1998-11-21.abstract: Soldiers in Jakarta, Indonesia, stop traffic to allow student demonstrators to mourn shooting deaths of several student demonstrators week ago; students are demanding that former Pres Suharto be put on trial; military promises investigation into whether live rounds had been fired after reports emerge that metal bullet fragments were found in several of victims' bodies (M)

1998-11-21.title: Progress Seen in Setting Up Key Ulster Council
1998-11-21.abstract: Prime Min Bertie Ahern of Irish Republic and David Trimble, first Minister of new Northern Ireland Assembly, say they made significant progress toward setting up council that would give Roman Catholic Ireland more influence in predominantly Protestant British province of Northern Ireland; under peace agreement approved in spring, cross-border council would havenorthern and southern officials cooperating on issues like tourism, agriculture and development; British Govt is to yield such powers next year; Ahern and Trimble say they made no progress on issue blocking work of new Assembly, disarming Irish Republican Army, and Trimble says accord will not work unless disarmament dispute is resolved (M)

1998-11-21.title: Mexico Rebels Emerge, and Start Complaining
1998-11-21.abstract: Top leaders of Mexico's Zapatista rebels, wearing trademark black ski masks, emerge from jungle hide-outs to appear in public for first time in two years; Indian rebels angrily complain that commission of federal lawmakers charged with organizing peace talks treated them 'like animals'; say many of 29 Zapatista delegates to three-day meeting had no beds; accuse lawmakers of being racist; Zapatistas demand apology but do not threaten to walk out of meetings (M)

1998-11-21.title: BAGHDAD DISRUPTS ARMS INSPECTIONS
1998-11-21.abstract: Iraq balks at handing over documents on chemical and biological weapons and missile systems, throwing up new roadblock to work of United Nations arms inspectors less than week after agreeing to cooperate; American and British officials warn that air strikes remain immediate option if Government of Pres Saddam Hussein does not honor its pledge to renew cooperation with inspectors; Iraq appears to believe it can balk on major issues pending comprehensive review of its relations with UN, but Security Council decided that review cannot take place until there is full cooperation with inspectors; confrontation has come sooner than expected; it raises questions about Hussein's understanding of seriousness of threat he faces and limited diplomatic help he can expect, if any, from former supporters like Russia, China and France (M)

1998-11-22.title: Clinton Appeals To North Korea For Closer Ties
1998-11-22.abstract: Pres Clinton, speaking at joint appearance with South Korean Pres Kim Dae Jung in Seoul, makes unusual direct appeal to North Korea to set aside any nuclear ambitions in favor of strengthening ties to South Korea and US; says construction site in North Korea that could be beginnings of nuclear weapons plant and test firing last Aug of missile over Japan are 'cause for deep concern'; Clinton also comments on Iraq's refusal to comply with request from weapons inspectors; he urges caution and says it is 'important that we not overreact'; photo (M)

1998-11-22.title: Bosnia Economy Still at Mercy of Political Leaders
1998-11-22.abstract: Bosnian political leaders are so good at resisting changes needed to revive economy that diplomats and foreign officials say their governments now face choice between providing large amounts of financial aid for years and watching country collapse; photo (M)

1998-11-22.title: Pinochet Case Spurring Debate on International Laws
1998-11-22.abstract: Many scholars and jurists regard unexpected arrest in London of Chilean dictator, Gen Augusto Pinochet, and request for his extradition by Spain as unusual chance to test high-minded human rights conventions that oblige countries to prosecute great atrocities but are invariably ignored; say case will test whether Britain is willing to lets its international human rights commitments prevail over domestic law giving immunity to heads of state (M)

1998-11-22.title: Lords Defy Commons And Labor Sees Red
1998-11-22.abstract: Britain's House of Lords has voted down and returned to House of Commons five times bill to change procedures in elections of British representatives in European Parliament; upper body has power only to delay, not defeat, legislation passed by Commons; real impulse behind rebellion is Prime Min Tony Blair's resolve to strip hereditary peers of their right to vote in Lords (M)

1998-11-22.title: Bad Faith Is Charged as Talks With Mexican Rebels Open
1998-11-22.abstract: Current talks between Zapatista rebels and mediators are not going well; there is no sign of peace between Mexican Government and rebels in Chiapas, and little reason to hope for settlment any time soon; map; photo (M)

1998-11-23.title: 'Leave the Guy Alone,' Troops Say as Clinton Visits Korea
1998-11-23.abstract: On a near-freezing pale-gray day on the tarmac of this U.S. Air Force base south of Seoul, President Bill Clinton did not have to offer any explanations for his conduct to GIs, most of them here on one-year tours without their families.

1998-11-23.title: Rancor Rules Out Progress At Peace Talks in Mexico
1998-11-23.abstract: Angry invective and rancor thwart progress between Mexican Govt mediators and top leaders of Zapatista rebels at two days of peace talks in Chiapas; maps (M)

1998-11-23.title: Students Raise Voices In Israel
1998-11-23.abstract: Israel's university students, who have well-earned reputation for indifference regarding political protests, are emerging as powerful voice for rearranging national priorities after month-long strike for lower tuition; are demanding shift from country's preoccupation with security to greater attention to social needs; have won support from labor unions and from unemployed and poor; photo (M)

1998-11-23.title: 13 Die in Muslim-Catholic Riots in Indonesia; Churches Looted
1998-11-23.abstract: Gang fight between Muslims and Christian migrants erupt into fierce rioting in Jakarta, Indonesia; six are killed and nearly dozen churches are burned or damaged; rioting, unrelated to political demonstrations and military gunfire that have kept city on edge for more than week, is sharp reminder of potential for violence as nation suffers through political turmoil and deep economic pain; photo (M)

1998-11-23.title: IRAQ RATCHETS UP RENEWED DEFIANCE OVER INSPECTIONS
1998-11-23.abstract: Iraq, week after averting military strikes by US, issues new series of defiant statements that it has little intention of cooperating with UN arms inspectors; remains on collision course with US; Foreign Min Mohammed Saeed al-Sahhaf rebuffs UN demand for more documents on country's arms program; reasserts that Baghdad has already produced more than two million pages of files and has no more documents to give; in second direct challenge in three days to US and Britain, Iraqis have in effect stated that they will step back only to October 31, when all cooperation with arms inspectors ceased, leaving all restrictions placed on monitors before final break in place; Clinton Administration warns US forces remain poised to act; national security adviser Samuel R Berger says Iraq's objection to request for documents is unreasonable and urges favorable response to UN request (M)

1998-11-24.title: Hygiene Poll Bares Source Of the French Je Sais Quoi
1998-11-24.abstract: Results of national surveys published in French daily, Le Figaro, finds 96 percent of French live in homes equipped with showers or baths in late 20th century, even more than those with bidets, but that only 47 percent bathe every day; found only 60 percent of Frenchman changed underwear daily--same percentage of all French people who regularly wash hands after going to toilet (S)

1998-11-24.title: 4 Brazilian Officials Resign As Bid-Rigging Scandal Erupts
1998-11-24.abstract: Four senior Brazilian Govt officials resign, scalded by accusations that two may have tried to influence bidding on giant telephone system; include Communications Min Luis Carlos Mendonca de Barros, brother Foreign Trade Sec Jose Roberto and Andra Lara Resende, president of Govt's National Bank of Economic and Social Development (S)

1998-11-24.title: Bankers Trust Deal Near, Deutsche Bank Confirms
1998-11-24.abstract: FRANKFURT—  Deutsche Bank AG said Monday it was in the final stages of forming the world's biggest financial-services firm with the acquisition of Bankers Trust Corp.

1998-11-24.title: Turks Are Furious Over Rebuff From Italy on Kurd Rebel Chief
1998-11-24.abstract: Turkish Govt and Turkish citizens are enraged over decision by Italy not extradite Kurdish rebel leader, Abdullah Ocalan, who was recently arrested in Rome; most view Ocalan, who has led 14-year uprising that has cost estimated 30,000 lives as diabolical figure; Italian laws forbids extradition of prisoners to countries that apply death penalty (M)

1998-11-24.title: World Briefing
1998-11-24.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: EURO'S SUPPORTERS SPEAK OUT  Urging their  Government to end its ambivalence toward the euro, 114 British business leaders said failure to join the single European currency ''would pose a serious threat to our future prosperity and to our influence in the world.'' Britain is among four European Union nations that will not use the euro, left, when the new currency is introduced in six weeks.   Alan Cowell (NYT)    BRITAIN: QUANTIFYING LIFE'S QUALITY  The Government announced plans to measure the quality of life using such factors as housing, traffic and air pollution in an attempt to establish whether life in Britain is getting better or worse. Deputy Prime Minister John Prescott said he wanted the new statistics to be used alongside traditional measures. ''The quality of life is not simply economic,'' Mr. Prescott said.   (Reuters)

1998-11-25.title: Vladimir P. Demikhov, 82, Pioneer in Transplants, Dies
1998-11-25.abstract: Vladimir P Demikhov, Russian surgeon whose pioneering work in performing organ transplants on dogs set stage for similar operations on humans in later years, dies at age 82 (M)

1998-11-25.title: Indonesia Panel to Study Reports Of Army Slayings in East Timor
1998-11-25.abstract: Indonesia's human rights commission will probe charges that soldiers executed up to 50 people in East Timor earlier in November in military operation against armed rebels; International Committee of Red Cross says team it sent to area found no confirmation of large-scale atrocities; as many as 2,000 East Timorese students continue to occupy local parliament building, Dili, to protest alleged killings (M)

1998-11-25.title: Security Council Hears Report on Iraq and Takes No Action
1998-11-25.abstract: Richard Butler, chief United Nations arms inspector, tells Security Council that he is disappointed with Iraq's cooperation with resumed inspections, particulary refusal to provide requested documents; Council agrees to continue discussing issue; critics and supporter of Iraq apparently agree Pres Saddam Hussein should be given time to show how he intends to proceed; United States tries to portray firm resolve after earlier signaling new policy of patience (M)

1998-11-25.title: Tiny Step to Help the Cease-Fire in Kosovo
1998-11-25.abstract: Ethnic Albanian rebels in Kosovo, Serbia, release Serbian police officer Goran Zbiljic after American diplomats intercede; release is seen tiny advance in effort to prop up fragile cease-fire, as international monitors help in small ways to defuse tensions; photo (M)

1998-11-25.title: South Africa Embroiled in Dispute Over Registration for '99 Vote
1998-11-25.abstract: Controversy is brewing in South Africa over first formal voter registration process since all-race elections began in 1994; fears are growing that administrative chaos could keep millions from voting; Independent Electoral Commission says it will delay registration drive in about half of nation, admitting it is not prepared to meet its optimistic goal of signing up 23 million to 25 million eligible voters in weekend; says it needs more time to train personnel to man registration booths (M)

1998-11-26.title: Centrist Ruler, Under Fire, Is Voted Out In Turkey
1998-11-26.abstract: Turkish Prime Min Mesut Yilmaz's Government, plagued by competing pressure from Islamic groups and secular military, loses confidence vote, 214-314; vote comes after Yilmaz is implicated in corruption scandal involving purchase by gangsters of state-owned bank; military is expected to retain ultimate political power as rivalries among parties make it unlikely that elections will produce stable Government (M)

1998-11-26.title: Former Ally Of Netanyahu May Return To Cabinet
1998-11-26.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu is ready to bring former Foreign Min David Levy back into his Cabinet in bid to shore up his disintegrating coalition and fortify his chances at either re-election or assembling unity Government; Levy, moderate who resigned in Jan, says he will return; Netanyahu and Foreign Min Ariel Sharon cancel trips abroad to concentrate on stabilizing Government (M)

1998-11-26.title: Beijing Says It Will Bid For Olympics Set for 2008
1998-11-26.abstract: Beijing says it wll bid for 2008 Summer Olympic Games; Mayor Jia Qinglin submits proposal to Chinese Olympic Committee, which will decide whether to apply to International Olympic Committee; importance of bid to Chinese leaders noted (M)i

1998-11-26.title: British Court Rules Against Pinochet; Now Cabinet Must Weigh Extradition
1998-11-26.abstract: British Law Lords, 3-2, reject former Chilean dictator Gen Augusto Pinochet's sovereign immunity claim and require him to remain in custody in London while Spain seeks his extradition on mass murder and genocide charges; Home Sec Jack Straw must now rule on matter; Amnesty International hails decision; photo (M)

1998-11-26.title: Europeans, But Not U.S., Rejoice At Ruling
1998-11-26.abstract: Many European leaders hail British ruling that former Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet can be extradited to Spain; United States officials and legal experts seem uneasy with idea that former Government leaders can be held responsible by foreign courts; United Nations Human Rights High Comr Mary Robinson sees ruling heartening human rights defenders around world; photo (M)

1998-11-27.title: Valentin M. Berezhkov, 82, Interpreter for Stalin at Talks
1998-11-27.abstract: Valentin M Berezhkov, Russian who interpreted for Stalin and Molotov at conferences that they, Pres Franklin D Roosevelt and Winston Churchill attended during World War II, dies at age 82 (M)

1998-11-27.title: Coolio Thrills German Fans With Gangsta Or Theft Rap
1998-11-27.abstract: American rap star Coolio arrives at local courthouse in Boblingen, Germany, to face trial on charges that he stole $900 worth of clothes and assaulted store clerk at local boutique; Coolio claims it was misunderstanding, and his lawyers seek out-of-court settlement; police cordon off street in front of courthouse as streets are jammed with hundreds of Coolio's teen-age fans (M)

1998-11-27.title: World Briefing
1998-11-27.abstract: Interior Min Sergei Stepashin says Russian authorities want new powers to check election candidates for criminal connections, after killing of Galina Starovoitova, liberal member of Parliament (S)

1998-11-27.title: Montreal Journal; Canadians, Estranged, Even in Their Pop Culture
1998-11-27.abstract: Quebec is divided from rest of Canada, and not just by its use of French language and desire of many to secede from Canada; Quebecers smoke more, get divorced more and are said to be more stressed out than people in rest of Canada; basic difference is way Quebecers see their country; Claude Meunier, host of wildly popular television program in Quebec, says Quebecers are eager for their French language and culture to survive; photo (M)

1998-11-27.title: Spanish Judge Is Hoping To See Secret Files in U.S.
1998-11-27.abstract: Spanish judge who wants former Chilean dictator, Augusto Pinochet, to stand trial, plans to go to Washington to try to see secret Government files; Judge Baltasar Garzon is particularly interested in United States Government files about events of 1970's, when Central Intelligence Agency and other American Embassy personnel were close to Pinochet Government and knew of activities of its powerful secret police; that police force is widely considered responsible for worst excesses of Pinochet Government, including kidnapping, torture and secret execution of leftist opponents (M)

1998-11-28.title: WORLD BRIEFING
1998-11-28.abstract: EUROPE    YUGOSLAVIA: KIDNAPPED REPORTERS RELEASED -- Rebels in Kosovo freed two Serbian reporters who were kidnapped 40 days ago and also released two moderate ethnic Albanian politicians. The Kosovo Liberation Army called the releases a good-will gesture.   (Reuters)    RUSSIA: MOVE TO BLOCK COMMUNISTS -- Galvanized by last week's murder of the legislator Galina V. Starovoitova, prominent reformers announced plans to form a coalition to prevent the Communists from returning to power. The coalition includes former Prime Ministers Yegor Gaidar and Sergei V. Kiriyenko, and former Deputy Prime Ministers Boris Y. Nemtsov and Anatoly B. Chubais. In the past, Russian liberals have been torn apart by rivalry.   (AP)

1998-11-28.title: A POLICY STRUGGLE STIRS WITHIN NATO
1998-11-28.abstract: Clinton Adm presents NATO with far-reaching program that seeks to define what alliance has become since cold war ended, and sets out its goals for next century; but proposals are running into European opposition that threatens to undermine 50th-anniversary NATO summit meeting in Washington in April; NATO diplomats say agenda for meeting is intended partly to convince Congress that NATO is still central to American security, but it has raised European concerns that United States is trying to turn orgn into freewheeling global vehicle for its own interests (M)

1998-11-28.title: Javier Garcia Paniagua, 61, Mexican Ruling Party Loyalist
1998-11-28.abstract: Javier Garcia Paniagua, former senior Mexican official whose hard-line views aligned him with group within governing Institutional Revolutionary Party known as 'dinosaurs,' dies at age 61; photo (M)

1998-11-28.title: Israel Is Rethinking Its Stand on Lebanon
1998-11-28.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu cut short a trip abroad to reconsider the Israeli Army's 20-year occupation of southern Lebanon after the killing of seven Israeli soldiers there in the last week and a half.

1998-11-28.title: Gently, China Flexes Strategic Muscles in Asia
1998-11-28.abstract: SINGAPORE—  Outlining a strategic blueprint for Asia that differs sharply from the approach of the United States, Defense Minister Chi Haotian of China said Friday that security in the region could not be achieved by military alliances.

1998-11-29.title: African Nations Agree on Cease-Fire for Congo by December
1998-11-29.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac says leaders of Congo and neighboring nations, in Paris for French-African summit meeting, met with United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan and agreed to cease-fire in revolt in Congo by mid-Dec; does not say whether rebels, who were not invited to summit, were consulted; Annan met with Congo Pres Laurent Kabila and leaders of Angola and Zimbabwe, who back him, and leaders of Uganda and Rwanda, who back rebels (M)

1998-11-29.title: Blair Finds Autonomy Only Whets Scots' Appetite
1998-11-29.abstract: Scottish Nationalists, campaigning for election to new Scottish Parliament on platform of independence from United Kingdom, are attracting far more support than expected; are seriously challenging Labor Party's traditional domination of Scotland, forcing Prime Min Tony Blair to take bigger role in campaign; map; photo (M)

1998-11-29.title: Purges Hint at Beginning of the End for Milosevic
1998-11-29.abstract: Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic, in what may be sign of weakness, has purged his innermost circle, dismissing heads of army, air force and intelligence service and trusted political commissar; purge culminated with dismissal of Army Staff Chief Gen Momcilo Perisic; Milosevic is seen by former associates as increasingly insecure, even paranoid, as Kosovo drifts from his control, economy weakens and he bows to demands of his politically powerful wife, Mirjana Markovic, replacing his apparatchiks with hers; photo (M)

1998-11-29.title: Police Action In '97 Haunts Government In Canada
1998-11-29.abstract: Treatment of protesters during 1997 Asia-Pacific Economic Coooperation summit meeting, Vancouver, has turned into recurring political nightmare for Canadian Prime Min Jean Chretien's Government, which allegedly ordered police to be aggressive in protection former Indonesia Pres Suharto from embarrassment; inquiry into incident has been postponed; Solicitor Gen Andy Scott has resigned after being overheard talking about probe with fellow passenger on plane (M)

1998-11-29.title: Spanish Judge Investigating Rights Abuses In Argentina
1998-11-29.abstract: Spanish Judge Baltazar Garzon, who seeks extradition of former Chilean dictator Gen Augusto Pinochet, is also probing role of Emilio E Massera, retired Argentine admiral, and other former Latin American military officers in Chilean military operation that sought capture of Chilean exiles abroad and their return to Chile for execution; inquiry has spurred Bolivian opposition to demand local probe of Pres Hugo Banzer, former military dictator; Argentine Pres Carlos Saul Menem, who pardoned Massera and other military officers involved in political kidnappings in Argentina, backs Chile's stand that former military leaders should be tried in own nations and opposes extradition of Argentine military officers; Massera is under house arrest in Argentina in case involving seizure of newborn infants from mothers who were then apparently executed (M)

1998-11-30.title: Chilean Official Says His Country Will Pursue Justice Against Pinochet
1998-11-30.abstract: Chile's Foreign Min Jose Miguel Insulza presses effort to persuade Britain to free Gen Augusto Pinochet, stating that Chile will pursueits own justice against former dictator; says popular view that 83-year-old Pinochet has unlimited immunity in Chile is wrong, adding that there 14 cases against him initiated by alleged victims of his dictatorship that are in hands of prosecutors in Santiago (M)

1998-11-30.title: Shifts in Symbol and Substance Muddy Quebec Vote
1998-11-30.abstract: Reversal of symbols on campaign posters for election in Quebec is one of many reversals in election that has distracted voters, confounded experts and added layer of thick ambiguity to one of continent's most baffling political landscapes; signs that prominently feature blue and white fleur-de-lis flag, provocative symbol of Quebec separatist movement, currently belongs to anti-separatist Liberal Party and its leader Jean Charest; Charest, former leader of federal Conservative Party, who arrived in Quebec as great federalist hope to banish separatists, seems to have gone from hero to goat, with latest polls showing Liberals headed for stinging defeat; photos; map (M)

1998-11-30.title: Soviet Mindset Blocks Capitalism on Farms
1998-11-30.abstract: Agriculture remains least reformed sector of Russian economy, despite vaunted talk of building free market; nation's chronically inefficient collective farms have been relabeled rather than reorganized as Govt has refused to approve sale of farmland and has done little to help fiercely conservative farmers cope with brave new world of capitalism; distress has been compounded by virtual collapse of banking system and ruinous drought; harvests are at lowest point in 45 years and production of staples continues to plummet--leaving country heavily dependent on imports; conditions at state-owned collective near Novogorod described; photos; map (M)

1998-11-30.title: Congo Rebel Chief, Citing Lack Of Details, Holds Back on Truce
1998-11-30.abstract: Ernest Wamba dia Wamba, leader of rebels on Congo, says he has not heard about cease-fire agreement announced in Paris and is therefore unable to commit to it; says fighting will continue until details become clear (S)

1998-11-30.title: Slavs and Albanians Form Unusual Coalition in Macedonia
1998-11-30.abstract: Leaders of Macedonia's most radical ethnic political parties, former enemies, are forming coalition to govern fragile former Yugoslav republic as war in neighboring Kosovo has underscored what could happen if they fail; Arben Xhaferi, leader of ethnic Albanians, is optimistic both sides can find common ground for ethnic integration through mutual understanding; is negotiating with nationalist leader Ljupco Georgievski, slated to be new Prime Minister; country's ethnic Albanians feel strongly that they are victims of systematic discrimination by Government, and foreign diplomats concur; are generally poorest, have worst social services and hold proportionally far fewer jobs in public sector (M)
